castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 2 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator, Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. also on screen: Character_The_Old_Man. THE OLD MAN sleeps. THE NARRATOR's eye gleams in the gap. Fingers undo the lantern a hair-hinges creak. A single thin ray seeks the pillow. The filmed eye is closed. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "much that a single thin ray fell upon the vulture eye.". Character_The_Narrator still wears dark wool coat, black trousers, soft-soled shoes Camera directive: Close-up tracking the single thin lantern ray as it snakes across darkness onto the pillow. Performance: Acting intensity 8/10: Feverish intense stare with dilated pupils and clenched jaw Optics: f/1.8 shallow depth of field, isolated ray highlight Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, heavy grain, near-black shadows, thin warm amber lantern ray
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.
- Character_The_Old_Man [The Old Man]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Frail elderly man, thin stooped build, sparse white hair,....

CAST COUNT: exactly 2 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator, Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "much that a single thin ray fell upon the vulture eye.". Start speaking immediately with "much" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient/Foley: hinges creak. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. also on screen: Character_The_Old_Man. THE OLD MAN sleeps. THE NARRATOR's eye gleams in the gap. Fingers undo the lantern a hair-hinges creak. A single thin ray seeks the pillow. The filmed eye is closed. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "I undid the lantern cautiously - oh, so cautiously - cautiously ( for the hinges creaked )- just so". Character_The_Narrator still wears dark wool coat, black trousers, soft-soled shoes Camera directive: Extreme macro on fingers easing lantern shutter, 100mm lens, hinges creak under cautious pressure. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Narrowed hyper-focused eyes, parted lips in extreme caution Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field, soft bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, heavy grain, near-black shadows, thin warm amber lantern ray
